text.title: Async Python Patterns
text.summary: asyncio.gather for concurrent I/O, TaskGroup for structured concurrency, Semaphore for rate limiting, and timeouts.

rule.statement: Use asyncio.gather() for independent concurrent I/O operations. Sequential await calls run one at a time; gather runs them concurrently, reducing total latency proportionally.
rule.rationale: Ten 0.5s API calls take 5s sequentially; with gather they take ~0.5s. The benefit scales with the number of independent I/O operations.

example.code: async def process_batch(items: list[str]) -> list[str]: results = [] async with asyncio.TaskGroup() as tg: for item in items: task = tg.create_task(process_item(item)) # All tasks completed; collect results return [task.result() for task in ...]

# asyncio.wait_for (all Python versions) try: result = await asyncio.wait_for(slow_operation(), timeout=2.0) except asyncio.TimeoutError: ...
# asyncio.timeout context manager (Python 3.11+) async with asyncio.timeout(2.0): result = await slow_operation()
